steps.title: Identify what both texts agree on
steps.content: Text 1 says cassava’s “rise to popularity was slow” — Portuguese traders brought it to the West African coast in the 1500s, but inland communities had little contact with the coast, so cassava remained relatively unknown until the 1800s. Text 2 also describes cassava’s “slow adoption into the diet of West Africans.” Both texts agree on one central fact: cassava took a very long time to become important in West African diets after it was introduced.

steps.title: Test each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Cassava did not become a significant crop in West Africa until long after it was first introduced.': Both texts explicitly state that cassava’s rise was slow — Text 1 pins introduction to the 1500s and significance to the 1800s; Text 2 calls its adoption slow. This is the point both authors share. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Several of the most commonly grown crops in West Africa are originally from Brazil.': Only cassava is discussed; neither text makes any claim about “several” crops from Brazil. ✗
  • Choice C — 'The climate of the West African coast in the 1500s prevented cassava’s spread in the region.': Neither text mentions climate as a factor; Text 1 cites limited inland-coastal contact and Text 2 cites preparation knowledge. ✗
  • Choice D — 'The most commonly used methods to cook cassava today date to the 1500s.': Neither text describes cooking methods or makes any claim about when they originated. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
